top_signals.title: US Consumer Navigating Shocks 'Remarkably Well,' Says Michelle Meyer
top_signals.summary:
Elevated energy prices have stoked inflation fears, raising the risk that the Federal Reserve and other central banks may need to keep rates elevated.
Michelle Meyer, Chief Economist and Head of the Economics Institute at Mastercard, discusses how the consumer is faring amid an increasingly uncertain economic picture, especially as summer travel picks up. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.
